item.title: Does easier access to alcohol affect road safety? Evidence from Texas municipalities
item.description: We examine the impact of expanded local alcohol access on traffic safety, exploiting close local option elections in Texas as a source of quasi-random variation. Legalization between 2003 and 2019 increases liquor licenses, driven almost entirely by off-premise retailers, and reallocates alcohol sales toward retail outlets without increasing total crashes, DUI crashes, or fatal crashes.

item.title: Science under collective bargaining: How unionization shapes academic labor markets and research productivity
item.description: We study how faculty and graduate-student unionization changes sorting, employment, wages, and research productivity in U.S. academic labor markets. Using staggered union recognition and contract ratification across universities, together with legislative timing as an instrument for union exposure, the project estimates how collective bargaining shapes incentives and innovation in a high-skill knowledge sector.

item.title: Legalized Sports Betting and College Performance
item.description: This project estimates the causal impact of legalized sports betting on college students' academic performance and engagement, using quasi-random variation in exposure to live sporting events generated by individual fandom and course-specific academic schedules at Purdue University.

item.title: Contact Information
item.description: Mailing and office addresses at Purdue University.

item.title: Palette Preview
item.description: Temporary comparison page for potential minimalist color schemes.
